methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $700M - $3.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
LeydenJar Technologies is a Series B climate and energy company that has raised €23M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies: KoBold Metals (Series C, $3.0B), Electric Hydrogen (Series C, $1.0B), and Amogy (Series C, $700M). The broader comparable universe includes one public company and eight private peers spanning the climate and energy technology landscape.

The peer-median method is the applicable triangulation approach here. The three selected comparables yield a median valuation of $1.0B, with a range spanning $700M to $3.0B. This method anchors the analysis by benchmarking LeydenJar against directly relevant companies at similar or slightly advanced funding stages within the same sector.

The triangulated valuation range is $850M to $1.1B, with a median of $1.0B. This relatively narrow band reflects reliance on a single methodological input; given only one applicable method, the range is necessarily constrained. Key uncertainties include the stage discount appropriate for Series B relative to the Series C peers, technology differentiation within the peer set, and capital efficiency metrics not reflected in valuation alone. Investors should weight this analysis alongside LeydenJar's specific technical progress, market timing, and capital requirements relative to comparable companies' trajectories.
